Klasse DifficultyStrategy

java.lang.Object
difficulties.DifficultyStrategy
Bekannte direkte Unterklassen:
EasyDifficulty, HardDifficulty, MediumDifficulty
public abstract class DifficultyStrategy extends Object
The DifficultyStrategy class serves as an abstract representation of the decision-making logic for determining moves and removals in a game. It provides methods and utility functionality to evaluate and execute strategies based on the current game state and opponent behavior. Subclasses implement specific difficulty levels by overriding the abstract methods for move calculation and piece removal.

    Methode
    Beschreibung
    protected Node[]
    blockBeginnerTrap(List<Node[]> playerMoves, ImaginativePlayer player)
    Identifies and executes a move to block an opponent's potential trap during the setting phase of the game.

    Identifies a potential move to block the opponent from forming a mill.
    protected Node[]
    blockZwickmill(List<Node[]> playerMoves, List<Node[]> opponentMoves, ImaginativePlayer opponent)
    Identifies a potential move to block an opponent's "Zwick Mill" formation.
    abstract Node[]
    calculateMove(ImaginativePlayer player, ImaginativePlayer opponent)
    Calculates the optimal move for a player based on a specific difficulty strategy.
    abstract Node
    calculateRemoval(ImaginativePlayer player, ImaginativePlayer opponent)
    Determines the node from which a stone should be removed as part of the strategy.

    Identifies a potential move for clustering stones based on the player's strategy.

    Creates a map entry containing a tactic name and its corresponding move generation function.

    Identifies all "almost mill" positions for the given player.
    protected Node[]

    Searches through a list of player moves to identify a mill formation for the specified player.
    protected Node[]
    findZwickMill(List<Node[]> playerMoves, ImaginativePlayer player)
    Identifies a potential "Zwick Mill" move for the specified player.

    isInMill(int id, int[] mill)
    Checks if the given id is present in the specified mill array.
    protected Node[]
    openMill(List<Node[]> playerMoves, ImaginativePlayer player, ImaginativePlayer opponent)
    Determines and returns a move that opens a mill by relocating a player's stone while ensuring that the target node does not have an opponent's stone as a neighbor.
    protected Node[]
    randomMove(List<Node[]> moves)
    Selects a random move from the given list of moves.
    protected Node
    randomRemoval(List<Node> removals)
    Randomly selects and removes a node from the provided list of removable nodes.
    protected Node
    removeMillThreat(List<Node> removableStones, List<Node[]> opponentMoves, ImaginativePlayer opponent)
    Identifies and returns the most strategic stone to remove from the opponent.
    protected final Node[]
    tryMoves(Map.Entry<String,Supplier<Node[]>>... tactics)
    Iterates through a list of move-generating tactics and returns the first non-null move set produced by any of the tactics.
    protected Node[]
    useFiggerMill(List<Node[]> playerMoves, ImaginativePlayer player)
    Attempts to find a move for the given player that completes an almost-formed mill by transferring a stone from another fully-formed mill.

  • Felddetails

    • random

      private final Random random

  • Konstruktordetails

    • DifficultyStrategy

      public DifficultyStrategy()

  • Methodendetails

    • calculateMove

      public abstract Node[] calculateMove(ImaginativePlayer player, ImaginativePlayer opponent)
      Calculates the optimal move for a player based on a specific difficulty strategy. This method generates a set of possible moves
      Siehe auch:
      • EasyDifficulty
      • MediumDifficulty
      • HardDifficulty

    • calculateRemoval

      public abstract Node calculateRemoval(ImaginativePlayer player, ImaginativePlayer opponent)
      Determines the node from which a stone should be removed as part of the strategy. This method is intended to analyze the game state and decide on the optimal removal of an opponent's stone, adhering to the rules and the strategy being implemented.
      Parameter:
      player - The player executing the removal.
      opponent - The opponent whose stone might be removed.
      Gibt zurück:
      The node representing the stone to be removed, or null if no valid removal is found.
      Siehe auch:
      • EasyDifficulty
      • MediumDifficulty
      • HardDifficulty

    • tryMoves

      @SafeVarargs protected final Node[] tryMoves(Map.Entry<String,Supplier<Node[]>>... tactics)
      Iterates through a list of move-generating tactics and returns the first non-null move set produced by any of the tactics. If a tactic is successful, the corresponding tactics name is logged.
      Parameter:
      tactics - An array of map entries, where each entry consists of a string representing the strategy's name and a supplier that generates a potential move set.
      Gibt zurück:
      An array of Node representing the first valid move set found, or null if no strategy produces a valid move.

    • entry

      protected Map.Entry<String,Supplier<Node[]>> entry(String name, Supplier<Node[]> tactic)
      Creates a map entry containing a tactic name and its corresponding move generation function.
      Parameter:
      name - The name of the tactic.
      tactic - A supplier that provides an array of Node representing possible moves for the tactic.
      Gibt zurück:
      A map entry where the key is the tactic name and the value is the supplier function for move generation.

    • isInMill

      private boolean isInMill(int id, int[] mill)
      Checks if the given id is present in the specified mill array.
      Parameter:
      id - The id to check for in the mill array.
      mill - An array of integers representing the mill.
      Gibt zurück:
      true if the id is found in the mill array, false otherwise.

    • randomMove

      protected Node[] randomMove(List<Node[]> moves)
      Selects a random move from the given list of moves. The method retrieves one move array by randomly choosing an index from the provided list of possible moves.
      Parameter:
      moves - A list of arrays of Node, where each array represents a potential move set.
      Gibt zurück:
      An array of Node representing a randomly chosen move from the list.

    • findMill

      protected Node[] findMill(List<Node[]> playerMoves, ImaginativePlayer player)
      Searches through a list of player moves to identify a mill formation for the specified player. A mill consists of a set of nodes where the player has successfully aligned their stones according to the game's rules, as determined by the RuleManager.
      Parameter:
      playerMoves - A list of arrays of Node, each representing a potential move set for the player.
      player - The player whose moves are being analyzed for mill formation.
      Gibt zurück:
      An array of Node representing the first found mill for the player, or null if no mill is found.

    • blockMill

      protected Node[] blockMill(List<Node[]> playerMoves, List<Node[]> opponentMoves, ImaginativePlayer opponent)
      Identifies a potential move to block the opponent from forming a mill. The method evaluates the player's possible moves and compares them against the opponent's moves and their almost mill nodes to determine an optimal blocking strategy.
      Parameter:
      playerMoves - A list of arrays of Node, representing the possible moves for the current player.
      opponentMoves - A list of arrays of Node, representing the possible moves for the opponent player.
      opponent - The opponent player whose strategy is being anticipated.
      Gibt zurück:
      An array of Node representing the blocking move, or null if no such move exists.

    • findAlmostMills

      private List<Integer> findAlmostMills(ImaginativePlayer player)
      Identifies all "almost mill" positions for the given player. An "almost mill" is defined as a configuration where a player has two stones in a potential mill, and one unoccupied position could complete the mill.
      Parameter:
      player - The player for whom the almost mill positions are being determined.
      Gibt zurück:
      A list of integers representing the IDs of the unoccupied positions that could complete a mill for the player.

    • clusterMove

      protected Node[] clusterMove(List<Node[]> playerMoves, ImaginativePlayer player)
      Identifies a potential move for clustering stones based on the player's strategy. The method evaluates the provided moves to locate a node associated with the player that is near unoccupied nodes, prioritizing positions with neighboring stones that belong to the player.
      Parameter:
      playerMoves - A list of arrays of Node, where each array represents a potential move set for the player.
      player - The player whose move strategy is being analyzed and optimized.
      Gibt zurück:
      An array of Node representing the identified optimal move for clustering, or null if no such move is found.

    • findZwickMill

      protected Node[] findZwickMill(List<Node[]> playerMoves, ImaginativePlayer player)
      Identifies a potential "Zwick Mill" move for the specified player. A Zwick Mill refers to a situation where a player can form a mill by leveraging shared positions across multiple potential mills containing a single player's stone and no opponent's stones. This method evaluates the player's potential moves and determines an optimal move based on shared positions in the player's almost mills.
      Parameter:
      playerMoves - A list of arrays of Node, where each array represents a potential move set for the player.
      player - The player whose moves and potential mill configurations are being analyzed.
      Gibt zurück:
      An array of Node representing the identified "Zwick Mill" move, or null if no such move is found.

    • blockZwickmill

      protected Node[] blockZwickmill(List<Node[]> playerMoves, List<Node[]> opponentMoves, ImaginativePlayer opponent)
      Identifies a potential move to block an opponent's "Zwick Mill" formation. A "Zwick Mill" refers to a situation where the opponent can form a mill by leveraging shared positions across multiple potential mills. This method evaluates the player's possible moves and compares them against the opponent's moves and their "Zwick Mill" to determine an optimal blocking strategy.
      Parameter:
      playerMoves - A list of arrays of Node, representing the possible moves for the current player.
      opponentMoves - A list of arrays of Node, representing the possible moves for the opponent player.
      opponent - The opponent player whose strategy is being anticipated.
      Gibt zurück:
      An array of Node representing the blocking move, or null if no such move exists.

    • blockBeginnerTrap

      protected Node[] blockBeginnerTrap(List<Node[]> playerMoves, ImaginativePlayer player)
      Identifies and executes a move to block an opponent's potential trap during the setting phase of the game. This method examines specific corner positions on the board and determines if the opponent is attempting to control a critical area by placing stones. If a potential trap is detected, the method suggests a move for the player to block the trap.
      Parameter:
      playerMoves - A list of possible moves the player can make. Each move is represented as an array of Node[], where the first node represents the origin and the second node represents the destination of the move.
      player - The player making the decision. This must be an instance of ImaginativePlayer who is in the Phases.SettingPhase of the game and has placed stones fewer than or equal to 8.
      Gibt zurück:
      An array of Node[] representing the suggested move to block the opponent's trap. Returns null if no suitable blocking move is found or if the conditions for the method execution are not met.

    • useFiggerMill

      protected Node[] useFiggerMill(List<Node[]> playerMoves, ImaginativePlayer player)
      Attempts to find a move for the given player that completes an almost-formed mill by transferring a stone from another fully-formed mill. The method evaluates current player moves, identifies almost-completed mills, and prioritizes moves that can leverage stones already forming part of other mills.
      Parameter:
      playerMoves - a list of potential moves for the player, where each move is represented as an array of two nodes (source and destination)
      player - the imaginative player executing the move, including details such as game phase and placed stones
      Gibt zurück:
      an array of two nodes representing the move (source node and target node) to complete an almost-formed mill, or null if no suitable move is found

    • openMill

      protected Node[] openMill(List<Node[]> playerMoves, ImaginativePlayer player, ImaginativePlayer opponent)
      Determines and returns a move that opens a mill by relocating a player's stone while ensuring that the target node does not have an opponent's stone as a neighbor. This method primarily operates outside of the "SettingPhase" of the game.
      Parameter:
      playerMoves - a list of moves, where each move is represented as an array of two nodes. The first node is the current position, and the second node is the target position of the stone.
      player - the imaginative player for whom the move is being determined.
      opponent - the opposing imaginative player whose stones need to be avoided while opening a mill.
      Gibt zurück:
      the first valid move from the player's moves that opens a mill without an opponent's neighboring stone, or null if no such move is found.

    • randomRemoval

      protected Node randomRemoval(List<Node> removals)
      Randomly selects and removes a node from the provided list of removable nodes.
      Parameter:
      removals - the list of nodes eligible for removal
      Gibt zurück:
      the randomly selected and removed node

    • removeMillThreat

      protected Node removeMillThreat(List<Node> removableStones, List<Node[]> opponentMoves, ImaginativePlayer opponent)
      Identifies and returns the most strategic stone to remove from the opponent. Evaluates potential opponent moves and selects the removable stone whose removal reduces the highest threat by disrupting mills.
      Parameter:
      removableStones - A list of nodes representing stones that can be removed.
      opponentMoves - A list of opponent's potential moves, each represented as a two-element array of nodes.
      opponent - The imaginative player representing the opponent whose potential moves and threat levels are to be analyzed.
      Gibt zurück:
      The node representing the stone that should be removed to minimize threats, or null if no suitable stone is identified.
